titleStmt.title: Letter from Lord Hugh Gough to Augustine Birrell, 28 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Hugh Gough

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from Lord Hugh Gough (1849 - 1919) inquiring into what is happening in areas around Galway. As there are no ports, telegraphs or railways are open, Gough fears that 'regrettable events are occurring'. A note on the letter indicates that the letter was received by the General Secretary's Office quite late (10 May), and is therefore not being answered as it is presumed the county is now quiet.Lord Gough was appointed 3rd Viscount of Lough Cultra Castle, Gort. His grandfather, the 1st Viscount Gough, was a famed British Officer from the Anglo-Sikh wars. The letter was addressed to Augustine Birrell (1850 - 1933). Birrell was the chief secretary for Ireland from 1907 until his retirement in 1916 following the aftermath of the Easter Rising.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5611/7967/1

correspAction.placeName: Lough Curta Castel, Gort, County Galway

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
A.U.S.
This has only reached us now as Lord Gough
has since written to the
C. Sec. I am not answering
This letter. I presume all is
quiet now. APM 10/1/16 STATION & TELEGRAMS
GORT, 3 MILES. LOUGH CUTRA CASTLE, GORT, CO: GALWAY April. 28. 1916 My dear Sir, I should be obliged if you
would â provided there be no objection â
inquire into the proceedings since
Easter Monday, April 24, in the following
places near Lough Cutra viz: Athenry
Craughwell, Moyode Castle, and Kinvara.
No post or telegraph or railway is open
and there is good reason to think that
regrettable events are occurring.
I am
Yours faithfully
Gough
The Rt Hon ble Birrell M.P.
etc etc etc
Dublin Castle As Under Secretary
at this date
no notice seems
to be called
for. WML11/5 EOF
11.V.16
